description_long: This is a large, open-framed barn structure with a prominent gable roof supported by a series of evenly spaced rafters and beams. The frame is constructed from a grid of thick, rectangular posts and cross-bracing, creating a robust skeleton typical of traditional timber barns. The roof is steeply pitched, running the full length of the building, and the open truss system is clearly visible from all sides. The lower section features a regular arrangement of rectangular openings for doors and windows, with a large central doorway on the front and rear. The structure is devoid of exterior cladding, exposing the internal framework and allowing light to pass through. This barn is designed for maximum interior volume and accessibility, suitable for agricultural storage or livestock shelter.
description: A large, open-framed barn with a steep gable roof and exposed timber structure, featuring a grid of posts, beams, and wide door and window openings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, symmetrical framing, and the full depth of the roof trusses.
description_view_1: Side gable view highlighting the triangular roof profile and dense arrangement of rafters.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with a large central opening and visible roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side gable view, emphasizing the roof's pitch and the regular grid of the frame.
attribute_siding_pattern: exposed structural framing with no exterior siding
attribute_door_style: single large central hinged or sliding barn door on both front and rear
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple rectangular window openings arranged in a regular grid along the lower walls
